contract.name: v2/disaster/award/count.md
contract.description: Returns the count of awards that received disaster or emergency funding, filtered by required DEF codes and optional award type restrictions.

filter.description: Required wrapper for all filters. Must be a JSON object; null, arrays, or scalars trigger 422 missing-field errors. Backend ignores unrecognized keys both inside and outside this object.
filter.constraints: required; treat non-object values as missing; accepts additional properties without effect.

def_codes.description: Array of uppercase Disaster Emergency Fund codes. Validator accepts digits '1'-'9', single letters 'A'-'Z', and multi-letter codes surfaced in errors (e.g., AAA, AAB, AAL, QQQ).
def_codes.constraints: required; minItems=1; array members must be uppercase strings from the enumerated set; invalid or lowercase values return 400 with an allowed-values list.

award_type_codes.description: Optional array restricting results to awards linked through File D. Accepted values include the documented procurement and assistance codes plus the sentinel '-1' (which alone returns zero results).
award_type_codes.constraints: optional; null treated as absent; when provided must be a non-empty array whose members are within ['-1','02','03','04','05','06','07','08','09','10','11','A','B','C','D','IDV_A','IDV_B','IDV_B_A','IDV_B_B','IDV_B_C','IDV_C','IDV_D','IDV_E']; empty arrays return 422; invalid members return 400.

count.description: Total number of awards matching the supplied filters.

quirks: Endpoint only accepts POST; GET attempts return 405 with a method-not-allowed message.
Validation distinguishes between structural issues (422) and invalid values (400) using pipe-delimited field paths in error details.
Unrecognized keys at the request root or inside filter are silently ignored without affecting the count.
Award type sentinel '-1' is accepted despite being undocumented; combining it with other codes yields the other codes' results.
Shared filters such as time_period, keywords, and agencies are accepted but observed to have no effect on the returned count.
risks: Silent acceptance of unsupported filters can mislead clients into assuming filters were applied when results are unchanged.
Clients must handle both 422 (missing/bad structure) and 400 (invalid value) errors depending on the validation failure mode.
Reliance on the undocumented '-1' award type or assuming ignored filters have effect can lead to misinterpretation of zero or static counts.
gaps: The complete authoritative list of permitted DEF codes is inferred from error messages and may include additional values.
Documentation does not clarify whether ignoring shared filters like time_period is intentional or a defect.

probes.notes: Baseline request with required def_codes succeeds.
Future time_period accepted but count unchanged, indicating filter is ignored.
Invalid DEF code reveals the enumerated allowed set and triggers 400.
Undocumented '-1' award type is accepted but returns zero.

mismatches: Docs limit DEF codes to CARES entries, but validation accepts a broader set including digits and multi-letter codes such as AAA and QQQ.
Docs list award_type_codes without '-1', yet the API accepts '-1' (and even ignores null) for this filter.
gaps: The complete authoritative list of DEF codes may extend beyond those exposed via error messaging.
Documentation does not clarify whether ignoring shared filters like time_period or keywords is intentional.
risks: Silent acceptance of unsupported filters can mask configuration mistakes because counts remain unchanged.
Clients must branch on both 422 and 400 responses when handling validation failures.
Undocumented handling of award type '-1' can produce zero counts if relied upon incorrectly.
